jokes.text: Which put her wake-up time in the 3:00, 3:30 area.
jokes.setup: Lucille is already fully dressed and made up when Michael gets up early
jokes.punchline: The narrator's matter-of-fact calculation that she must have woken up at 3:00-3:30 AM
jokes.explanation: Classic AD narrator precision comedy - the specific time range makes Lucille's vanity absurd but believable

jokes.text: Lucille had gone over 25 times with Buster... and on a few occasions, had won Cutest Couple.
jokes.setup: Mother-son dinner dance called Motherboy
jokes.punchline: They've won 'Cutest Couple' - romantic category for mother and son
jokes.explanation: Perfectly establishes the inappropriate mother-son dynamic that drives the series. The casual delivery of something deeply disturbing.

jokes.text: As one entered sexual maturity, and the other one left it... it became harder to win.
jokes.setup: They used to win Cutest Couple
jokes.punchline: Narrator's clinical description of aging out of inappropriate attraction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant euphemistic description that makes the inappropriate relationship both clinical and deeply uncomfortable. Peak AD writing.

jokes.text: If you were hot, Mother, we would win.
jokes.setup: Lucille says 'I'm so hot'
jokes.punchline: Buster interprets it as physical attractiveness in context of couple's contest
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster moment - completely inappropriate but delivered with innocent directness

jokes.text: That could have something to do with the fact that a seal ate his hand. - I don't know what it is. - I think that's what it is. - Who knows? - [Narrator] It was that.
jokes.setup: Lucille wondering why Buster is mopey
jokes.punchline: Michael's obvious answer, Lucille's denial, and narrator's definitive confirmation
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of obvious truth vs. willful ignorance, capped by narrator's deadpan confirmation

jokes.text: I mean, look how he zips now.
jokes.setup: Lucille asks to be zipped up
jokes.punchline: Visual of Buster struggling to zip with his hook prosthetic
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that emphasizes Buster's handicap while showing Lucille's callousness

jokes.text: The Bluth children had been uneasy about missing limbs... since their father used a one-armed man to teach them lessons. That's why you don't yell.
jokes.setup: Michael won't spend time with one-handed Buster
jokes.punchline: Flashback to traumatic one-armed man lessons from childhood
jokes.explanation: Classic AD callback that recontextualizes earlier absurdity while explaining current character behavior

jokes.text: Of course they have father-daughter dances. - They do? He never took me? It was before we did your nose.
jokes.setup: Lindsay realizes father-daughter dances exist
jokes.punchline: Lucille's casual revelation about Lindsay's nose job timing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille cruelty - casual revelation of why her daughter was deemed unworthy of attention

jokes.text: He had even gotten a vanity plate made up announcing the achievement.
jokes.setup: Tobias got cast as Frightened Inmate Number Two
jokes.punchline: Visual of vanity plate 'ANUSTART' (A New Start)
jokes.explanation: Iconic visual pun that works on multiple levels - Tobias's obliviousness to the sexual innuendo is perfect

jokes.text: And this wasn't the first time.
jokes.setup: Tobias made a vanity plate for a small role
jokes.punchline: Visual montage of multiple vanity plates for tiny roles
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing this is a pattern, not just one delusional moment

jokes.text: Actually, George Sr. was secretly living... in the attic above them and was starved for company- - Nellie. Now- who wants to take their top off?
jokes.setup: Narrator explains George Sr. is hidden in attic
jokes.punchline: Cut to him playing with dolls, asking them to take their tops off
jokes.explanation: Absurd visual that shows how isolation has affected George Sr. - creepy but played for comedy

jokes.text: That's a cross. - Across from where?
jokes.setup: Maeby asks about gold necklaces with 'T' on them
jokes.punchline: Her misunderstanding of what 'cross' means
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective wordplay that shows Maeby's ignorance about Christianity

jokes.text: Hey, there's booze in this.
jokes.setup: George Michael mentions going on a Christian camping trip
jokes.punchline: George Sr. discovers alcohol in what should be Christian materials
jokes.explanation: Simple hypocrisy gag about Christian camp materials containing alcohol

jokes.text: I had to take all pumps out of here a long time ago.
jokes.setup: Kids looking for air mattress pump
jokes.punchline: George Sr. removed all pumps for suicide prevention reasons
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about suicide prevention delivered casually - shows George Sr.'s mental state

jokes.text: Okay, I should've never taken the pumps out of here.
jokes.setup: George Sr. gives relationship advice about not letting women control you
jokes.punchline: Immediately realizes the irony - he removed pumps because of women's control
jokes.explanation: Great self-awareness moment where George Sr. realizes his hypocrisy immediately

jokes.text: Gob had once gotten married on a dare... a fact he'd only remembered when he was served with divorce papers.
jokes.setup: Gob needs to talk about his divorce
jokes.punchline: He forgot he was married until served with divorce papers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob characterization - so irresponsible he forgets major life events

jokes.text: I like to look in the mirror.
jokes.setup: Michael questions how Gob didn't notice his wife's body during sex
jokes.punchline: Gob's narcissistic admission about looking at himself during intimacy
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Gob - perfectly narcissistic and inappropriate, delivered with complete sincerity

jokes.text: [Narrator] They didn't, but it would have been.
jokes.setup: Gob says sex with his wife was disgusting
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals they never had sex but confirms it would have been disgusting
jokes.explanation: Classic AD narrator intervention - provides information while making a judgment, very economical

jokes.text: Up top. No?
jokes.setup: Barry talks about getting Buster's Swatch back from inside the seal
jokes.punchline: Asks for high-five, realizes Buster has no hand
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag playing on Buster's disability - uncomfortable but appropriately awkward

jokes.text: I swore I'd not go reality.
jokes.setup: Carl asks Tobias to play himself
jokes.punchline: Tobias's pretentious refusal to do reality TV
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tobias's delusional acting standards despite his complete lack of success

jokes.text: Did you know that you can get a refill on any drink you want here, and it's free? - It's a wonderful restaurant.
jokes.setup: Carl goes to get a new soda
jokes.punchline: His excitement about free refills as if it's a secret, narrator's enthusiastic agreement
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy for Carl Weathers - treats basic restaurant feature as amazing discovery

jokes.text: Let's rock-paper-scissors for it. - No! You would've-You would've won that, so-
jokes.setup: They need to decide who goes first at checkers
jokes.punchline: Buster realizes rock-paper-scissors is impossible with his hook hand
jokes.explanation: Nice subtle joke about practical limitations of having a hook hand

jokes.text: Can I open a can of soup for you? - Can it open a can? - Can what open a can? Oh, God!
jokes.setup: Lucille offers to open soup
jokes.punchline: George Michael's confusion about 'can it' vs 'can what', leading to awkward realization
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that builds to uncomfortable realization about Buster's limitation

jokes.text: Actually, I just wanted to spend some time with my Knuckle Buster.
jokes.setup: Awkward moment about Buster's hook
jokes.punchline: Lucille's nickname playing on 'knuckles' and 'Buster'
jokes.explanation: Mild pun that shows Lucille trying to make light of the situation inappropriately

jokes.text: No, he was going south. South to Motherboy.
jokes.setup: George Michael thinks he's going north to Promise Land
jokes.punchline: Dramatic reveal he's going south to Motherboy instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with dramatic reveal - shows Lucille's deception effectively

jokes.text: Or we could make sure it's the right seal first? - I mean, why get his hopes up? - Yeah, that's better.
jokes.setup: They should call Buster about finding the seal
jokes.punchline: Brothers' immediate agreement to avoid involving Buster
jokes.explanation: Shows the brothers' continued avoidance of dealing with Buster's trauma

jokes.text: Monster! - Hey, Brothers. - Bonster!
jokes.setup: Buster appears suddenly
jokes.punchline: Brothers' scared reaction, then Gob's portmanteau of 'Buster' and 'monster'
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay showing how Buster's appearance now startles people

jokes.text: There's not enough room for you and me and the stump and the thing- stuff.
jokes.setup: Gob trying to exclude Buster from the car
jokes.punchline: Gob's tactless reference to Buster's missing hand as 'stump'
jokes.explanation: Typical Gob insensitivity - shows his complete lack of social awareness

jokes.text: I'm havin' a- [Bleeps] tea party. What does it look like? I live here.
jokes.setup: Tobias catches George Sr. with dolls
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s defensive sarcasm about obvious doll play
jokes.explanation: Good defensive reaction that acknowledges the absurdity while maintaining George Sr.'s machismo

jokes.text: Stop licking my hand, you horse's ass.
jokes.setup: George Sr. playing with dolls
jokes.punchline: Talking to doll as if it's real and misbehaving
jokes.explanation: Shows how isolation has affected George Sr.'s mental state - treating dolls as real

jokes.text: I get those names confused. Apologies. Apologies all around.
jokes.setup: George Sr. mentions not wanting to be played like 'Polly or Nellie'
jokes.punchline: Realizes he just used feminine names for his dolls
jokes.explanation: Self-aware moment where George Sr. realizes he's exposed his doll-naming system

jokes.text: He couldn't wait to swoop in and throw on that sailor suit, because he's not damaged goods.
jokes.setup: Buster talking about George Michael going to Motherboy
jokes.punchline: Self-aware comment about being 'damaged goods'
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment from Buster about his perceived value

jokes.text: Left flipper. - So, the seal lost his hand too. Yeah, poor guy's probably out there without a flipper... swimming around in a circle, freaking out his whole family.
jokes.setup: They find the seal lost its flipper to a shark
jokes.punchline: Buster's empathy for the seal's similar situation
jokes.explanation: Beautiful parallel between Buster and the seal - both victims of family dysfunction and overprotection

jokes.text: You both were too sheltered to learn how to protect yourselves... and we can't let that happen to George Michael.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the captive-raised seal
jokes.punchline: Michael's realization about family pattern of overprotection
jokes.explanation: Key thematic moment connecting the seal metaphor to the Bluth family dynamics

jokes.text: Oh- [Bleeps] me. The exact same color. What are the odds?
jokes.setup: Lucille and George Michael arrive at Motherboy
jokes.punchline: Another mother-son pair wearing identical outfits
jokes.explanation: Shows the competitive nature of Motherboy and Lucille's investment in winning

jokes.text: Motherboy was also a heavy metal band... That used to rock pretty hard in the '70s. We are legally obligated to make the distinction.
jokes.setup: Reference to Motherboy event
jokes.punchline: Narrator's legal disclaimer about the real band
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke acknowledging real-world legal concerns in a deadpan way

jokes.text: You don't need the calories.
jokes.setup: Tobias now has George Sr.'s 'raw power'
jokes.punchline: Takes away Lindsay's food jar aggressively
jokes.explanation: Shows Tobias's new aggressive personality from studying George Sr.

jokes.text: For as it is written... you shall be with whom you have formed a more perfect union with under God.
jokes.setup: Maeby giving relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Fake biblical-sounding nonsense about relationships
jokes.explanation: Good fake-religious speak from Maeby trying to play Christian counselor

jokes.text: Take me with you! No, please! Please! - It's the wrong child.
jokes.setup: Michael and Buster try to rescue George Michael
jokes.punchline: Wrong child desperately wants to be rescued from Motherboy
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag showing other kids also want to escape this creepy event

jokes.text: And yet, anything goes at bath time. [Chuckles]
jokes.setup: Buster explaining Lucille's modesty rules
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of inappropriate bath time behavior
jokes.explanation: Deeply inappropriate revelation delivered casually - peak cringe comedy

jokes.text: Both things he just said were lies.
jokes.setup: Gob says they had sex and he's not a good liar
jokes.punchline: Narrator immediately contradicts both claims
jokes.explanation: Perfect narrator intervention - economical and reveals the irony of Gob's statement

jokes.text: What were you doin' there? - Wow. You should be the lawyer.
jokes.setup: Barry mentions catching the judge at a drag club
jokes.punchline: Gob's question backfires as good cross-examination technique
jokes.explanation: Good reversal where Gob accidentally demonstrates good legal thinking

jokes.text: Either I zip down or he zips up! And that is a mighty long zipper on Mother's Cher jumpsuit. You have to get on your knees to start it.
jokes.setup: Buster planning to zip-line to George Michael
jokes.punchline: TMI about helping Lucille with her Cher costume zipper
jokes.explanation: Inappropriate oversharing that makes the rescue plan even more uncomfortable

jokes.text: Whoa. This guy's straight? - Yeah. Then what the hell am I wearin' these for?
jokes.setup: Discussion about Tobias being straight
jokes.punchline: Dave Attell confused about wearing cutoff jean shorts for straight character
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about Tobias's perceived sexuality through his clothing choices

jokes.text: There's dozens of us. Dozens!
jokes.setup: Costume person suggests wearing jean shorts under pants
jokes.punchline: Tobias's passionate defense of never-nude community size
jokes.explanation: Iconic Arrested Development moment - Tobias's defensive pride about his rare condition became a major meme

jokes.text: If that man's straight, then I am sober.
jokes.setup: Dave Attell expressing disbelief about Tobias being straight
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating joke about his own drinking problem
jokes.explanation: Great guest star moment - Dave Attell using his real persona for the joke

jokes.text: You lost a lot of weight in Iraq, especially up in the northern region.
jokes.setup: Gob meeting his wife for first time he remembers
jokes.punchline: Confuses weight loss with breast reduction, wrong war zone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment - clueless, inappropriate, and geographically wrong

jokes.text: Wow. I mis-underestimated you.
jokes.setup: Gob willing to lie about consummation
jokes.punchline: Double-negative malapropism showing she's as confused as Gob
jokes.explanation: Good malapropism that shows why these two ended up together

jokes.text: Is this your son? - No, I'm his grandmother. - He's an orphan.
jokes.setup: Judge asks about George Michael
jokes.punchline: Lucille's lie escalates from grandmother to orphan for sympathy votes
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Lucille's competitive manipulation

jokes.text: Well, that little bit of honesty helped nothing.
jokes.setup: George Michael ruins sympathy lie by saying he has a dad
jokes.punchline: Lucille's frustration with honesty as tactical error
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille line - treats honesty as a strategic mistake rather than virtue

jokes.text: Maybe 'Operation Hot Mother.' - No, let's try to top that. - [Narrator] They never did.
jokes.setup: Brothers trying to name their rescue mission
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals they never improved on Buster's suggestion
jokes.explanation: Classic AD structure - builds expectation then deflates it with narrator efficiency

jokes.text: You know, you're as good a brother as you ever were.
jokes.setup: Michael apologizing to Buster
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment suggesting Buster was never that good
jokes.explanation: Subtle dig disguised as encouragement - very Michael

jokes.text: George Michael still has a chance. Take him!
jokes.setup: Buster stuck in the curtain during rescue
jokes.punchline: Tells them to save George Michael while he's trapped
jokes.explanation: Buster's heroic sacrifice parody, maintaining his childish dramatic flair

jokes.text: I'm Peter Pan, and you're Captain Hook. We'll pretend it's a costume.
jokes.setup: George Michael offering to dance with hook-handed Buster
jokes.punchline: Creative reframing of disability as costume theme
jokes.explanation: Beautiful moment showing George Michael's kindness and creativity in helping Buster feel normal

jokes.text: And I saw some of those people. That was a very competitive category. - Oh, don't I know it.
jokes.setup: Michael mentions Buster won 'Saddest' at Motherboy
jokes.punchline: Buster's pride in winning a depressing category
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Buster finding validation in any recognition

jokes.text: There's no way to tell who this man is. - That's me, Your Honor. I- [Bleeps] my wife.
jokes.setup: Judge claims photo evidence is unclear
jokes.punchline: Gob immediately identifies himself and confesses
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment - snatches defeat from jaws of victory with unnecessary honesty

jokes.text: The only thing more terrifying... than the escaped lunatic's hook was his twisted call! - Hey, campers. - [Kids Screaming]
jokes.setup: Maeby telling scary campfire story about hook-handed killer
jokes.punchline: Buster appears with actual hook, terrifying the kids
jokes.explanation: Brilliant convergence of classic campfire story with Buster's actual condition - perfect timing and setup

jokes.text: I'm a monster! This may have been a step backwards.
jokes.setup: Buster scares children at Christian camp
jokes.punchline: Narrator's clinical assessment of Buster's progress
jokes.explanation: Great understatement about Buster traumatizing children - typical AD narrator dryness
